title: Fencing Additional Information
description: Please provide detailed information about your fencing project

primary.summary: Comprehensive fencing requirements including 4 specific fence types, permitted locations, height restrictions, and approval requirements. All fences require ARB application and approval.
keyRequirements: All fences require application and approval by the ARB
4 fence types permitted: Type 1 (4.5' black ornamental aluminum), Type 2 (6' tan vinyl privacy), Type 3 (6' white vinyl with open pickets), Type 4 (3' white picket front yard)
Chain link fences prohibited except at dog park
Fence locations vary by lot type and must comply with specific restrictions
Fence must not encroach into neighboring property
primary.quote: All fences require application and approval by the ARB... Chain link fences are prohibited except at dog park.

additionalReferences.summary: Specific fence type requirements based on lot location - lakefront lots only Type 1, perimeter lots have specific requirements, traditional lots have different options
keyProvisions: Lakefront and Conventional Corner Lots - Type 1 only permitted
Back to Back Lots - Phase 1: Type 2 permitted in rear (except Lots 47-48), Phase 2: Type 1 or 2 permitted
Traditional Type 1 Lots - Type 3 (rear/side yards), Type 4 (front yard) permitted
Side yard fencing depths limited to 20 feet from back corner of home

additionalReferences.summary: ARB approval required for fences within easements, and property owner responsible for replacement if damaged during utility maintenance
additionalReferences.quote: ARB approval is required for fences and plantings erected within easements by the Owner/Builder. Replacement of fences, landscaping or any other improvements within easements is the responsibility of the Property Owner.

additionalReferences.summary: Required documentation and approval process for all improvements including fences, with refundable performance deposit requirements
keyRequirements: Completed Design Review Application Form required
Site plan must show all fencing locations with elevations
90 days to bring work into conformance after non-compliance notice

fields.label: Type of Fence
options: Type 3 - White Vinyl with Open Pickets (6')
Type 4 - White Wide Picket Front Yard (3')
fields.description: Select from approved Markland fence types. Chain link prohibited except at dog park.

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.7B - There are 4 different fence types at Markland
relevantBylaws.note: Each lot type has specific fence type restrictions - check your lot classification

fields.label: Fence Height
fields.description: Height must match approved fence type specifications

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.7B - Specific heights defined for each fence type
relevantBylaws.requirement: Fence heights are fixed by type and cannot be modified without ARB approval

fields.label: Fence Material and Color
fields.placeholder: Must match approved specifications: Type 1 (black aluminum), Type 2 (tan vinyl), Type 3 (white vinyl), Type 4 (white picket)
fields.description: Materials and colors are predetermined by fence type selection

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.7A & 7.7B - Exhibit 7.7A and Exhibit 7.7B for fence specifications
relevantBylaws.requirement: Colors and materials are fixed by fence type and cannot be customized

fields.label: Total Linear Footage
fields.description: Total length of fence to be installed

fields.label: Your Lot Type
fields.description: Your lot type determines which fence types are permitted

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.7B - Permitted locations for fence types vary at Markland
keyRestrictions: Traditional Lots 61-69: Type 3 required between driveway and rear of home
Side yard fencing limited to 20 feet from back corner of home

fields.label: Fence Location on Property
fields.placeholder: Specify front yard, back yard, side yard, and specific boundaries. Note any special restrictions for your lot type.
fields.description: Location restrictions apply based on lot type - check relevant bylaws

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.7B & 7.7C - Location restrictions by lot type
keyRequirements: Side yard fencing depths limited to 20 feet from back corner of home
Fences must not encroach into neighboring property
Fences must interface structures at 90° angles

fields.label: Distance from Property Line
fields.placeholder: Distance in feet from property boundary (minimum 3 feet required)
fields.description: Must maintain minimum setbacks and not encroach on neighbor's property

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.6B & 7.7G - Site wall and fence setback requirements
relevantBylaws.quote: In no case shall any site wall be built within three (3) feet of a property line... Fences constructed alongside yards must not encroach into the neighboring property.

fields.label: Property Survey Confirmation
fields.description: Accurate boundary identification required to prevent encroachment

fields.label: Neighbor Notification
fields.description: Good neighbor policy recommended for shared boundary fences

fields.label: Gates Included
fields.description: Will the fence include any gates?

fields.label: Gate Details
fields.placeholder: Describe gate locations, sizes, and access purposes. Gates must match fence specifications.
fields.description: Gates must be consistent with approved fence type and style

fields.label: Utility Access Considerations
fields.placeholder: How will utility companies access meters, lines, or equipment?
fields.description: Critical for easement areas - utility access must be maintained

relevantBylaws.requirement: Easement areas must remain accessible for utility maintenance, and fence owner responsible for repairs if damaged during utility work

fields.label: Installation Method
fields.description: Installation must meet design standards regardless of installer

fields.label: Contractor Information
fields.description: Required if using professional installation

fields.label: Installation Timeline
fields.description: Must coordinate with ARB approval and performance deposit requirements

relevantBylaws.requirement: 90 days from correspondence to bring work into conformance with Design Standards

fields.label: Building Permit Status
fields.description: Check local requirements - ARB approval required regardless of permit status

fields.label: ARB Application Status
fields.description: ARB approval required before any fence installation

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.7A - All fences require application and approval by the ARB
relevantBylaws.process: Submit completed Design Review Application Form with refundable performance deposit

fields.label: HOA Guidelines Compliance
fields.description: Must comply with specific fence type requirements for your lot classification

fields.label: Fence Installation Requirements
fields.placeholder: Confirm fence top level installation, proper interface with house at 90° angles, and landscape buffering requirements
fields.description: Must meet specific installation standards

keyRequirements: Fence top must be horizontally level, not slope with grade
Fences must interface structures at 90° angles and terminate at sidewalls
All fences must be complemented with appropriate landscape buffering

fields.label: Drainage Impact
fields.placeholder: Will the fence affect water drainage or runoff? Consider swale and easement impacts.
fields.description: Fence installation cannot disrupt drainage patterns or easement functions

fields.label: Maintenance Plan
fields.placeholder: How will you maintain the fence over time? Include cleaning, repairs, and replacement schedules.
fields.description: Owner responsible for ongoing maintenance and compliance with design standards

fields.label: Architectural Harmony
fields.placeholder: How does the selected fence type complement your home's architecture and neighborhood character?
fields.description: Fence selection must be appropriate for architectural style and community aesthetics

relevantBylaws.quote: ARB will be evaluating each proposed improvement application for total effect... It is possible that a proposed improvement might meet individual criteria and still not receive approval if its overall aesthetic impact is unacceptable.

required_documents: Completed Design Review Application Form (required by ARB)
Site plan showing fence location, setbacks, and elevations
Refundable Performance Deposit (amount per Application Form)
Fence type specifications (must match approved types)
Contractor license and insurance (if using contractor)

criticalReminders: NO fence installation allowed without ARB approval
Fence types are strictly defined - custom designs require special approval
Lot type determines which fence types are permitted
Chain link fences prohibited (except dog park)
Performance deposit required and refundable upon compliance
commonViolations: Using wrong fence type for lot classification
Failing to maintain level fence top
